revision_history.description: New data model format and notation; add IdentifyType

cluster_id.name: Identify
cluster_id.summary: Used to make a device physically identifiable. Its behavior is influenced by the IdentifyTypeEnum, which specifies the presentation method (e.g., light, beep, display).

classification.summary: This cluster is a fundamental, general-purpose utility operating at the device endpoint level, providing core identification functionality.

values.name: None
values.summary: No presentation.

values.name: LightOutput
values.summary: Light output of a lighting product.

values.name: VisibleIndicator
values.summary: Typically a small LED.

values.name: Display
values.summary: Presentation will be visible on display screen.

values.name: Actuator
values.summary: Presentation will be conveyed by actuator functionality such as through a window blind operation or in-wall relay.

values.name: Blink
values.summary: e.g., Light is turned on/off once.

values.name: Breathe
values.summary: e.g., Light is turned on/off over 1 second and repeated 15 times.

values.name: Okay
values.summary: e.g., Colored light turns green for 1 second; non-colored light flashes twice.

values.name: ChannelChange
values.summary: e.g., Colored light turns orange for 8 seconds; non-colored light switches to the maximum brightness for 0.5s and then minimum brightness for 7.5s.

values.name: FinishEffect
values.summary: Complete the current effect sequence before terminating. e.g., if in the middle of a breathe effect (as above), first complete the current 1s breathe effect and then terminate the effect.

values.name: StopEffect
values.summary: Terminate the effect as soon as possible.

values.name: Default
values.summary: Indicates the default effect is used

attributes.name: IdentifyTime
attributes.summary: Represents remaining time (seconds) endpoint identifies itself. Non-zero value initiates identification state (RECOMMENDED flashing light 0.5s period), decrementing every second. Reaching or setting to 0 terminates identification state.

attributes.name: IdentifyType
attributes.summary: Indicates how identification state is presented to the user. SHALL contain one of the values defined in IdentifyTypeEnum. 'None' SHALL NOT be used if device capable of other methods.

commands.name: Identify
commands.summary: This command starts or stops the receiving device identifying itself by setting the IdentifyTime attribute.

fields.name: IdentifyTime
fields.summary: The value to set the IdentifyTime attribute to, representing the duration in seconds for the device to identify itself. A value of 0 stops identification.

commands.name: TriggerEffect
commands.summary: This command allows the support of feedback to the user, such as a certain light effect, providing visual feedback under specific circumstances.

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: This field SHALL indicate the identify effect to use and SHALL contain one of the non-reserved values in EffectIdentifierEnum. All values of the EffectIdentifierEnum SHALL be supported.

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.summary: This field SHALL indicate which variant of the effect, indicated in the EffectIdentifier field, SHOULD be triggered. If a device does not support the given variant, it SHALL use the default variant.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
if device.supports_variant(EffectVariant_field) then
    execute_effect(EffectIdentifier_field, EffectVariant_field)
else
    execute_effect(EffectIdentifier_field, default_variant)

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute whose value SHALL be the highest revision number in the cluster's revision history table (not defined in this section).

references.name: Data Model Specification
references.description: Base types like enum8 and uint16 are defined in the Matter Data Model Specification.

references.name: ClusterRevision attribute
references.description: A global attribute mentioned in the revision history, likely defined in the Matter Data Model Specification.

references.name: IdentifyTypeEnum
references.description: An enumeration defining the possible ways an identification state can be presented to the user, used by the IdentifyType attribute.

references.name: Interaction Model
references.description: Core Matter specification for command invocation and interaction layer mechanisms.

references.name: Groups Cluster
references.description: Manages endpoint membership in groups and group-related operations, including group IDs scoped to the accessing fabric.

references.name: Message Layer
references.description: Underlying Matter communication layer responsible for mechanisms like configuring group addresses for outgoing commands.

revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; CCB 1745 2100
New data model format and notation

cluster_id.name: Groups
cluster_id.summary: Manages the node-wide Group Table per endpoint. Supports discovering, adding, and removing endpoint membership from fabric-scoped groups. Can store client-set name strings for assigned groups.

classification.summary: A Base Utility cluster scoped to the Endpoint. It manages the node-wide Group Table, enabling operations like adding, removing, and discovering endpoint membership in groups, with all operations fabric-scoped.

features.name: GroupNames
features.summary: The ability to store a name for a group when a group is added. This feature indicates the ability to store a name for a group.

values.name: GroupNames
values.summary: The ability to store a name for a group.

attributes.name: NameSupport
attributes.summary: This attribute provides legacy, read-only access to whether the Group Names feature is supported. The most significant bit, bit 7 (GroupNames), SHALL be equal to bit 0 of the FeatureMap attribute (GN Feature). All other bits SHALL be 0.

commands.name: AddGroup
commands.summary: Allows a client to add group membership for the server endpoint. The GroupName field is ignored if the server does not support group names.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Identifies the group and any associated key material to which the server endpoint is to be added.

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: A human-readable name for the group. If no name, set to empty string. Ignored if server does not support group names.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
if not server.supports_group_names then ignore GroupName
status := SUCCESS
if not command_fields.are_valid then status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR
else if node.requires_security_material and not security_material.exists_for(GroupID) then status := UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS
else if server_endpoint.is_member_of(GroupID) then
  if server.supports_group_names then group.name[GroupID] := GroupName
  status := SUCCESS
else if not resources.available_to_add_membership then status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
else
  server_endpoint.add_membership_to(GroupID)
  if server.supports_group_names then group.name[GroupID] := GroupName
  status := SUCCESS
  if GroupID.was_previously_added and GroupName.is_provided and server.supports_group_name_storage then
    GroupTable.update_group_name(GroupID, GroupName)
if command.is_unicast then generate AddGroupResponse(Status := status)
else if command.is_groupcast then do_not_generate AddGroupResponse

commands.name: ViewGroup
commands.summary: Allows a client to request that the server responds with a ViewGroupResponse command containing the name string for a particular group.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Identifies the group for which the name is requested.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
status := SUCCESS
if not command_fields.are_valid then status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR
else if server_endpoint.is_member_of(GroupID) then status := SUCCESS
else status := NOT_FOUND
if command.is_unicast then generate ViewGroupResponse(Status := status)
else if command.is_groupcast then do_not_generate ViewGroupResponse

commands.name: GetGroupMembership
commands.summary: Allows a client to inquire about the group membership of the server endpoint, in a number of ways.

fields.name: GroupList
fields.summary: A list of group IDs to query. If empty, all group memberships are requested.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
response_group_ids := []
if GroupList.is_empty then
  response_group_ids := server_endpoint.get_all_member_group_ids()
else
  for each group_id in GroupList do
    if server_endpoint.is_member_of(group_id) then
      response_group_ids.add(group_id)
if GroupList.is_not_empty and response_group_ids.is_empty then
  if command.is_unicast then generate GetGroupMembershipResponse(GroupList := [])
  else do_not_generate GetGroupMembershipResponse
else
  generate GetGroupMembershipResponse(GroupList := response_group_ids)

commands.name: RemoveGroup
commands.summary: Allows a client to request that the server removes the membership for the server endpoint, if any, in a particular group.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Identifies the group from which membership is to be removed.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
status := SUCCESS
if not command_fields.are_valid then status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR
else if server_endpoint.is_member_of(GroupID) then
  server_endpoint.remove_membership_from(GroupID)
  status := SUCCESS
else status := NOT_FOUND
if command.is_unicast then generate RemoveGroupResponse(Status := status)
else if command.is_groupcast then do_not_generate RemoveGroupResponse
if ScenesManagementCluster.is_supported_on_endpoint then
  ScenesManagementCluster.remove_scenes_associated_with(GroupID)

commands.name: RemoveAllGroups
commands.summary: Allows a client to direct the server to remove all group associations for the server endpoint.
commands.effect_on_receipt:
server_endpoint.remove_all_group_memberships()
if command.is_unicast and not response.is_suppressed then
  generate response(Status := SUCCESS)
if ScenesManagementCluster.is_supported_on_endpoint then
  ScenesManagementCluster.remove_all_scenes_except_for_group_id(0)

commands.name: AddGroupIfIdentifying
commands.summary: Allows a client to add group membership in a particular group for the server endpoint, on condition that the endpoint is identifying itself.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Identifies the group and any associated key material to which the server endpoint is to be added.

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: A human-readable name for the group. If no name, set to empty string. Ignored if server does not support group names.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
if not server.supports_group_names then ignore GroupName
status := SUCCESS
if not server.is_currently_identifying then status := SUCCESS
else if not command_fields.are_valid then status := CONSTRAINT_ERROR
else if node.requires_security_material and not security_material.exists_for(GroupID) then status := UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS
else if server_endpoint.is_member_of(GroupID) then status := SUCCESS
else if not resources.available_to_add_membership then status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
else
  server_endpoint.add_membership_to(GroupID)
  if server.supports_group_names then group.name[GroupID] := GroupName
  status := SUCCESS
  if GroupID.was_previously_added and GroupName.is_provided and server.supports_group_name_storage then
    GroupTable.update_group_name(GroupID, GroupName)
if command.is_unicast and (status != SUCCESS or not response.is_suppressed) then
  generate response(Status := status)

commands.name: AddGroupResponse
commands.summary: Sent by the Groups cluster server in response to an AddGroup command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section of the AddGroup command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the GroupID field of the received AddGroup command.

commands.name: ViewGroupResponse
commands.summary: Sent by the Groups cluster server in response to a ViewGroup command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section of the ViewGroup command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the GroupID field of the received ViewGroup command.

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: If status is SUCCESS and group names are supported, set to the group name associated with that group in the Group Table; otherwise it is set to the empty string.

commands.name: GetGroupMembershipResponse
commands.summary: Sent by the Groups cluster server in response to a GetGroupMembership command.

fields.name: Capacity
fields.summary: Remaining capacity of the Group Table. 0: No further groups; >0 & <0xFE: Number of groups; 0xFE: At least 1; null: Unknown.

fields.name: GroupList
fields.summary: Contains group IDs of which the server endpoint is a member, filtered by the received GetGroupMembership command's GroupList if not empty.

commands.name: RemoveGroupResponse
commands.summary: Generated by the server in response to the receipt of a RemoveGroup command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section of the RemoveGroup command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the GroupID field of the received RemoveGroup command.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute whose value SHALL be the highest revision number in the cluster's revision history table. Not fully defined in this section.

references.name: FeatureMap
references.description: Global attribute representing a bitmap of supported optional features for the cluster. Not defined in this section.

references.name: Message Layer mechanisms
references.description: External mechanisms used for configuring group addresses for outgoing commands, distinct from the Group Table managed by this cluster.

references.name: underlying interaction layer
references.description: The layer that contains the node-wide Group Table, which the Groups cluster manages.

references.name: Group Table
references.description: A node-wide table, part of the underlying interaction layer, managed by the Groups cluster to store group memberships for endpoints.

references.name: Data Model Spec
references.description: Base types like map8 are defined in the Matter Data Model Specification.

references.name: FeatureMap attribute
references.description: A standard attribute in Matter clusters. Bit 0 (GN Feature) is referenced as being equal to NameSupportBitmap bit 7 for the NameSupport attribute.

references.name: Interaction Model
references.description: Command invocation (Core Spec)

references.name: Identify Cluster
references.description: Used to control identifying functionality, referenced by AddGroupIfIdentifying command.

references.name: Scenes Management Cluster
references.description: Provides attributes and commands for setting up and recalling scenes. Referenced by RemoveGroup and RemoveAllGroups commands for scene removal.

revision_history.description: Updated from ZCL Scenes Cluster: Cluster ID to 0x0062, name to Scenes Management, removed provisional status. Removed SceneCount, CurrentScene, CurrentGroup, SceneValid, NameSupport attributes. Removed Explicit, TableSize, FabricScenes features. Removed EnhancedAddScene/Response, EnhancedViewScene/Response. TransitionTime field now uses milliseconds in AddScene, ViewSceneResponse, RecallScene.

cluster_id.name: Scenes Management
cluster_id.summary: Manages scenes, allowing devices to store and recall predefined states. Based on the ZCL Scenes Cluster, it enables grouping device states for later activation.

classification.summary: A fundamental application-level cluster operating at the endpoint scope, indicating standard functionality for scene management.

features.name: SceneNames
features.summary: This feature indicates the ability to store a name for a scene when a scene is added. If supported, a scene can have an associated name.

Dependencies.title: Mandatory Groups Cluster Implementation
Dependencies.trigger_condition: An endpoint implements the Scenes Management server cluster
Dependencies.effect: The endpoint SHALL also implement the Groups server cluster.
Dependencies.summary: If an endpoint provides Scenes Management server functionality, it must also provide Groups server functionality. Pseudocode: IF implements(ScenesManagement.Server) THEN implements(Groups.Server)

Dependencies.title: Cascading Scene Removal from Groups Commands
Dependencies.trigger_condition: RemoveGroup or RemoveAllGroups command invoked on the Groups cluster
Dependencies.effect: Corresponding scenes are removed from the Scenes Management cluster.
Dependencies.summary: Commands to remove groups in the Groups cluster will also remove associated scenes in the Scenes Management cluster. Pseudocode: ON Groups.RemoveGroup(groupID) OR Groups.RemoveAllGroups() DO ScenesManagement.RemoveScenesForGroup(groupID)

Dependencies.title: Fabric Removal Cleans Up Scenes Data
Dependencies.trigger_condition: RemoveFabric command invoked on the Operational Credentials Cluster
Dependencies.effect: All scenes data for the associated fabric SHALL be removed from the Scene Table.
Dependencies.summary: When a fabric is removed via the Operational Credentials Cluster, all scene data belonging to that fabric is purged from the Scenes Management cluster. Pseudocode: ON OperationalCredentials.RemoveFabric(fabricID) DO ScenesManagement.RemoveAllScenesForFabric(fabricID)

Handling of fabric-scoping.rule: Attributes and commands are scoped to accessing fabric only
Operations without an accessing fabric SHALL fail
Handling of fabric-scoping.pseudocode: if accessing_fabric == null then return UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS

Handling of fabric-scoping.rule: Scenes with same Group ID + Scene ID across fabrics are fabric-isolated
Removing a fabric clears associated scenes
Scene Table capacity per fabric < half of SceneTableSize (max 253)
Handling of fabric-scoping.pseudocode: max_fabric_scenes := min(floor(SceneTableSize / 2), 253); if scene_table[fabric].count >= max_fabric_scenes then apply_resource_exhaustion_behavior()

values.name: CopyAllScenes
values.summary: Copy all scenes in the scene table

values.name: SceneCount
values.summary: This field SHALL indicate the number of scenes currently used in the server’s Scene Table on the endpoint where the Scenes Management cluster appears. This only includes the count for the associated fabric.

values.name: CurrentScene
values.summary: This field SHALL indicate the scene identifier of the scene last invoked on the associated fabric. If no scene has been invoked, the value of this field SHALL be 0xFF, the undefined scene identifier.

values.name: CurrentGroup
values.summary: This field SHALL indicate the group identifier of the scene last invoked on the associated fabric, or 0 if the scene last invoked is not associated with a group.

values.name: SceneValid
values.summary: Indicates if server state corresponds to CurrentScene/CurrentGroup. TRUE if valid, FALSE otherwise. Set to FALSE if a Scenes-designated attribute in another cluster is modified or if current scene is modified by RecallScene/StoreScene commands.

values.name: RemainingCapacity
values.summary: This field SHALL indicate the remaining capacity of the Scene Table on this endpoint for the accessing fabric. Note that this value may change between reads, even if no entries are added or deleted on the accessing fabric, due to other clients associated with other fabrics adding or deleting entries that impact the resource usage on the device.

values.name: AttributeID
values.summary: Present for all instances in ExtensionFieldSetStruct. Determines which Value* field is used based on attribute's data type. SHALL NOT refer to an attribute without the Scenes ("S") designation in the Quality column.

values.name: ValueUnsigned8
values.summary: Indicates attribute value in an extension field set. Used when the attribute indicated by AttributeID is of type bool, map8, or uint8. Mapping is determined by the attribute's base type.

values.name: ValueSigned8
values.summary: Data types int8 SHALL map to ValueSigned8.

values.name: ValueUnsigned16
values.summary: Data types map16 and uint16 SHALL map to ValueUnsigned16.

values.name: ValueSigned16
values.summary: Data types int16 SHALL map to ValueSigned16.

values.name: ValueUnsigned32
values.summary: Data types map32, uint24, and uint32 SHALL map to ValueUnsigned32.

values.name: ValueSigned32
values.summary: Data types int24 and int32 SHALL map to ValueSigned32.

values.name: ValueUnsigned64
values.summary: Data types map64, uint40, uint48, uint56 and uint64 SHALL map to ValueUnsigned64.

values.name: ValueSigned64
values.summary: Data types int40, int48, int56 and int64 SHALL map to ValueSigned64.

values.name: ClusterID
values.summary: This field SHALL indicate the cluster-id of the cluster whose attributes are in the AttributeValueList field.

values.name: AttributeValueList
values.summary: Indicates a set of attributes and their values stored as part of a scene. Attributes without the Scenes ("S") designation in their cluster specification SHALL NOT be used in this list.

values.name: SceneGroupID
values.summary: This field is the group identifier for which this scene applies, or 0 if the scene is not associated with a group.

values.name: SceneID
values.summary: This field is unique within this group, which is used to identify this scene.

values.name: SceneName
values.summary: The field is the name of the scene. If scene names are not supported, any commands that write a scene name SHALL simply discard the name, and any command that returns a scene name SHALL return an empty string.

values.name: SceneTransitionTime
values.summary: This field is the amount of time, in milliseconds, it will take for a cluster to change from its current state to the requested state.

values.name: ExtensionFields
values.summary: Refers to Scene Table Extensions. SHALL only use attributes with Scene quality. Each ExtensionFieldSetStruct holds attribute values for a cluster on the same endpoint with Scene ("S") designation. A scene aggregates these fields.

attributes.name: LastConfiguredBy
attributes.summary: Indicates the Node ID of the node that last configured the Scene Table. Null if not configured or unknown. The Node ID is scoped to the accessing fabric.

attributes.name: SceneTableSize
attributes.summary: Indicates the number of entries in the Scene Table on this endpoint, total across all fabrics. The minimum size is 16, unless a device type defines a larger value.

attributes.name: FabricSceneInfo
attributes.summary: Indicates a list of fabric scoped information about scenes on this endpoint. The number of list entries SHALL NOT exceed the number of supported fabrics by the device.

commands.name: AddScene
commands.summary: Allows a client to add a new scene or replace an existing one. Extension field sets may be omitted, including the case of no extension field sets at all.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Indicates the group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Indicates the scene identifier in the Scene Table.

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Indicates the transition time of the scene, measured in milliseconds.

fields.name: SceneName
fields.summary: Indicates the name of the scene.

fields.name: ExtensionFieldSetStructs
fields.summary: Contains the list of extension fields.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
set status := SUCCESS
if GroupID != 0 then
    if endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupID) == null then
        set status := INVALID_COMMAND
        goto generate_response
end if

if ExtensionFieldSetStructs is invalid according to ExtensionFieldSetStruct definition then
    set status := INVALID_COMMAND
    goto generate_response
end if

if SceneTable.exists(GroupID, SceneID) then
    // Scene will be replaced in next step
else if SceneTable.capacity_for_fabric(current_fabric) is exhausted then
    set status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
    goto generate_response
end if

SceneTable.add_or_replace_scene(GroupID, SceneID, TransitionTime, SceneName, ExtensionFieldSetStructs)
set status := SUCCESS

for each ExtensionFieldSetStruct in SceneTable.ExtensionFieldSetStructs do
    if ClusterID referenced by ExtensionFieldSetStruct is not implemented on endpoint then
        omit ExtensionFieldSetStruct from processing
    end if
    for each AttributeValuePairStruct in ExtensionFieldSetStruct do
        if AttributeID referenced by AttributeValuePairStruct is not implemented on endpoint then
            omit AttributeValuePairStruct from processing
        end if
    end for
end for

if ExtensionFieldSetStructs list has multiple entries with same ClusterID then
    keep only the last entry for that ClusterID
end if

if any ExtensionFieldSet contains same AttributeID more than once then
    keep only the last one for that AttributeID
end if

SceneTable.SceneTransitionTime := TransitionTime

generate_response:
if command_received_as_unicast then
    generate AddSceneResponse(Status := status, GroupID := GroupID, SceneID := SceneID)
end if

commands.name: AddSceneResponse
commands.summary: Generated in response to a received AddScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section for AddScene command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received AddScene command.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received AddScene command.

commands.name: ViewScene

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Indicates the group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Indicates the scene identifier in the Scene Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
set status := SUCCESS
set retrieved_scene_entry := null

if GroupID != 0 then
    if endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupID) == null then
        set status := INVALID_COMMAND
        goto generate_response
end if

if SceneTable.find(GroupID, SceneID) == null then
    set status := NOT_FOUND
    goto generate_response
end if

set retrieved_scene_entry := SceneTable.retrieve(GroupID, SceneID)
set status := SUCCESS

generate_response:
if command_received_as_unicast then
    generate ViewSceneResponse(
        Status := status,
        GroupID := GroupID,
        SceneID := SceneID,
        TransitionTime := if status == SUCCESS then retrieved_scene_entry.TransitionTime else omit,
        SceneName := if status == SUCCESS then retrieved_scene_entry.SceneName else omit,
        ExtensionFieldSetStructs := if status == SUCCESS then retrieved_scene_entry.ExtensionFieldSetStructs else omit
    )
end if

commands.name: ViewSceneResponse
commands.summary: Generated in response to a received ViewScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section for ViewScene command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received ViewScene command.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received ViewScene command.

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: If status is SUCCESS, copied from Scene Table entry; otherwise omitted.

fields.name: SceneName
fields.summary: If status is SUCCESS, copied from Scene Table entry; otherwise omitted.

fields.name: ExtensionFieldSetStructs
fields.summary: If status is SUCCESS, copied from Scene Table entry; otherwise omitted.

commands.name: RemoveScene

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Indicates the group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Indicates the scene identifier in the Scene Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
set status := SUCCESS
if GroupID != 0 then
    if endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupID) == null then
        set status := INVALID_COMMAND
        goto generate_response
end if

if SceneTable.find(GroupID, SceneID) == null then
    set status := NOT_FOUND
    goto generate_response
end if

SceneTable.remove(GroupID, SceneID)
set status := SUCCESS

generate_response:
if command_received_as_unicast then
    generate RemoveSceneResponse(Status := status, GroupID := GroupID, SceneID := SceneID)
end if

commands.name: RemoveSceneResponse
commands.summary: Generated in response to a received RemoveScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section for RemoveScene command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received RemoveScene command.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received RemoveScene command.

commands.name: RemoveAllScenes

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Indicates the group identifier in the Group Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
set status := SUCCESS
if GroupID != 0 then
    if endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupID) == null then
        set status := INVALID_COMMAND
        goto generate_response
end if

SceneTable.remove_all_scenes_for_group(GroupID)
set status := SUCCESS

generate_response:
if command_received_as_unicast then
    generate RemoveAllScenesResponse(Status := status, GroupID := GroupID)
end if

commands.name: RemoveAllScenesResponse
commands.summary: Generated in response to a received RemoveAllScenes command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section for RemoveAllScenes command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received RemoveAllScenes command.

commands.name: StoreScene
commands.summary: If a scene requires TransitionTime and/or SceneName, these must be set up by a prior AddScene command (e.g., with no scene extension field sets).

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Indicates the group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Indicates the scene identifier in the Scene Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
set status := SUCCESS
if GroupID != 0 then
    if endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupID) == null then
        set status := INVALID_COMMAND
        goto generate_response
end if

if SceneTable.exists(GroupID, SceneID) then
    // Scene exists, will be updated in next step
else if SceneTable.capacity_for_fabric(current_fabric) is exhausted then
    set status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
    goto generate_response
end if

if SceneTable.exists(GroupID, SceneID) then
    set scene_entry := SceneTable.find(GroupID, SceneID)
    scene_entry.ExtensionFieldSets := current_state_of_other_clusters_on_endpoint.ExtensionFieldSets
    // Other fields (TransitionTime, SceneName) remain unchanged
else
    SceneTable.add_new_entry(GroupID, SceneID)
    set scene_entry := SceneTable.find(GroupID, SceneID)
    scene_entry.SceneTransitionTime := 0
    scene_entry.SceneName := ""
    scene_entry.ExtensionFieldSets := current_state_of_other_clusters_on_endpoint.ExtensionFieldSets
end if
set status := SUCCESS

generate_response:
if command_received_as_unicast then
    generate StoreSceneResponse(Status := status, GroupID := GroupID, SceneID := SceneID)
end if

commands.name: StoreSceneResponse
commands.summary: Generated in response to a received StoreScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section for StoreScene command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received StoreScene command.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received StoreScene command.

commands.name: RecallScene
commands.summary: The transition time determines how long the transition takes from the old cluster state to the new cluster state. A gradual transition is recommended where possible, but the exact algorithm is implementation-defined.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Indicates the group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Indicates the scene identifier in the Scene Table.

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Indicates the transition time of the scene, measured in milliseconds.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
set status := SUCCESS
if GroupID != 0 then
    if endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupID) == null then
        set status := INVALID_COMMAND
        goto generate_response
end if

if SceneTable.find(GroupID, SceneID) == null then
    set status := NOT_FOUND
    goto generate_response
end if

set scene_entry := SceneTable.retrieve(GroupID, SceneID)
for each cluster on endpoint do
    set extension_field_sets := scene_entry.get_extension_field_sets_for_cluster(cluster.ID)
    if extension_field_sets is not null then
        for each attribute_value_pair in extension_field_sets do
            if attribute_value_pair.AttributeID is implemented on cluster then
                cluster.set_attribute(attribute_value_pair.AttributeID, attribute_value_pair.Value)
            else
                // Skip unknown/missing attribute
            end if
        end for
    else
        // Cluster state remains unchanged
    end if
end for
set status := SUCCESS

set effective_transition_time := null
if TransitionTime field is present and its value is not null then
    set effective_transition_time := TransitionTime
else
    set effective_transition_time := scene_entry.SceneTransitionTime
end if
// Apply gradual transition from old to new state over effective_transition_time (implementation-defined)

generate_response:
if command_received_as_unicast then
    generate response(Status := status)
end if

commands.name: GetSceneMembership
commands.summary: Used to get the scene identifiers within a certain group for the endpoint implementing this cluster.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Indicates the group identifier in the Group Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
set status := SUCCESS
if GroupID != 0 then
    if endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupID) == null then
        set status := INVALID_COMMAND
        goto generate_response
end if

set status := SUCCESS

generate_response:
if command_received_as_unicast then
    generate GetSceneMembershipResponse(
        Status := status,
        Capacity := SceneTable.remaining_capacity_for_fabric(current_fabric),
        GroupID := GroupID,
        SceneList := if status == SUCCESS then SceneTable.get_scene_ids_for_group(GroupID) else omit
    )
end if

commands.name: GetSceneMembershipResponse
commands.summary: Generated in response to a received GetSceneMembership command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section for GetSceneMembership command.

fields.name: Capacity
fields.summary: Remaining capacity of the Scene Table for the accessing fabric. Values: 0 (no more scenes), >0 & <0xFE (number of scenes), 0xFE (at least 1 more), null (unknown).

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received GetSceneMembership command.

fields.name: SceneList
fields.summary: If status is SUCCESS, contains identifiers of all scenes in the Scene Table with the corresponding Group ID; otherwise omitted.

commands.name: CopyScene
commands.summary: Allows a client to efficiently copy scenes from one group/scene identifier pair to another.

fields.name: Mode
fields.summary: Contains information on how the scene copy is to proceed. The CopyAllScenes bit (1=copy all scenes, ignore SceneIdentifierFrom/To; 0=copy single scene).

fields.name: GroupIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Identifier of the group from which the scene is to be copied. Uniquely identifies the source scene with SceneIdentifierFrom.

fields.name: SceneIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Identifier of the scene from which the scene is to be copied. Uniquely identifies the source scene with GroupIdentifierFrom.

fields.name: GroupIdentifierTo
fields.summary: Identifier of the group to which the scene is to be copied. Uniquely identifies the destination scene with SceneIdentifierTo.

fields.name: SceneIdentifierTo
fields.summary: Identifier of the scene to which the scene is to be copied. Uniquely identifies the destination scene with GroupIdentifierTo.

commands.effect_on_receipt:
set status := SUCCESS
if GroupIdentifierFrom != 0 or GroupIdentifierTo != 0 then
    if endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupIdentifierFrom) == null or endpoint.GroupTable.find(GroupIdentifierTo) == null then
        set status := INVALID_COMMAND
        goto generate_response
end if

if Mode.CopyAllScenes == 0 then
    if SceneTable.find(GroupIdentifierFrom, SceneIdentifierFrom) == null then
        set status := NOT_FOUND
        goto generate_response
    end if
end if

if Mode.CopyAllScenes == 1 then
    SceneTable.copy_all_scenes_from_group_to_group(GroupIdentifierFrom, GroupIdentifierTo)
else // Mode.CopyAllScenes == 0
    SceneTable.copy_scene(GroupIdentifierFrom, SceneIdentifierFrom, GroupIdentifierTo, SceneIdentifierTo)
end if

// If a scene already exists at destination, it is replaced (implicit in copy_scene/copy_all_scenes_from_group_to_group)

if SceneTable.capacity_for_fabric(current_fabric) is exhausted then
    set status := RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
    // Scenes already copied are kept
    goto generate_response
end if

set status := SUCCESS

generate_response:
if command_received_as_unicast then
    generate CopySceneResponse(Status := status, GroupIdentifierFrom := GroupIdentifierFrom, SceneIdentifierFrom := SceneIdentifierFrom)
end if

commands.name: CopySceneResponse
commands.summary: Generated in response to a received CopyScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Set according to the Effect on Receipt section for the CopyScene command.

fields.name: GroupIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received CopyScene command.

fields.name: SceneIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Set to the corresponding field of the received CopyScene command.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute for cluster version; its value SHALL be the highest revision number in the cluster's revision history table (not defined here).

references.name: ZCL Scenes Cluster
references.description: Original cluster from the Zigbee Cluster Library (ZCL) upon which the Matter Scenes Management Cluster is based (not defined here).

references.name: FeatureMap
references.description: Standard attribute representing a bitmap of supported optional features for a cluster (not fully defined here, only its bits are).

references.name: SceneCount
references.description: Attribute from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: CurrentScene
references.description: Attribute from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: CurrentGroup
references.description: Attribute from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: SceneValid
references.description: Attribute from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: NameSupport
references.description: Attribute from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: Explicit
references.description: Feature from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: TableSize
references.description: Feature from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: FabricScenes
references.description: Feature from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: EnhancedAddScene
references.description: Command from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: EnhancedAddSceneResponse
references.description: Command from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: EnhancedViewScene
references.description: Command from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: EnhancedViewSceneResponse
references.description: Command from the ZCL Scenes Cluster, removed in Matter Scenes Management.

references.name: AddScene
references.description: Command within Scenes Management cluster; its TransitionTime field was updated (not fully defined here).

references.name: ViewSceneResponse
references.description: Command within Scenes Management cluster; its TransitionTime field was updated (not fully defined here).

references.name: RecallScene
references.description: Command within Scenes Management cluster; its TransitionTime field was updated (not fully defined here).

references.name: Groups server cluster
references.description: Any endpoint implementing the Scenes Management server cluster SHALL also implement the Groups server cluster. Its RemoveGroup and RemoveAllGroups commands also remove scenes.

references.name: Operational Credentials Cluster
references.description: A cluster mentioned in the context of fabric-scoping, specifically for its RemoveFabric command which removes all scenes data for the associated fabric.

references.name: Data Model Specification
references.description: Base types (bool, int8, int16, int32, int64, map8, map16, map32, map64, string, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64, list) and fundamental identifiers (attribute-id, cluster-id, group-id, node-id) are defined in the Matter Data Model Specification.

references.name: ColorControl cluster
references.description: Mentioned in examples for AttributeValuePairStruct processing, e.g., CurrentX (AttributeID 0x0003), CurrentSaturation (Attribute 0x0001), ColorTemperatureMireds (Attribute 0x0007), EnhancedCurrentHue (Attribute 0x4000), EnhancedColorMode (Attribute 0x4001).

references.name: LevelControl cluster
references.description: Mentioned in examples for AttributeValuePairStruct processing, e.g., CurrentLevel (AttributeID 0x0000).

references.name: RecallScene command
references.description: Command used to modify the current scene by a fabric, affecting the SceneValid field.

references.name: StoreScene command
references.description: Command used to modify the current scene by a fabric, affecting the SceneValid field.

references.name: AddSceneResponse
references.description: Response type for the AddScene command.

references.name: AddScene command
references.description: Command mentioned in the Commands section.

references.name: RemoveFabric command
references.description: Command of the Operational Credentials Cluster used to remove fabric data.

references.name: RemoveGroup command
references.description: Command of the Groups cluster that removes scenes.

references.name: RemoveAllGroups command
references.description: Command of the Groups cluster that removes scenes.

references.name: UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS status code
references.description: Status code returned when no accessing fabric is available for an attribute read, write, or command invocation.

references.name: percent type
references.description: Derived type, treated as uint8 for mapping to ValueUnsigned8.

references.name: percent100ths type
references.description: Derived type, treated as uint16 for mapping to ValueUnsigned16.

references.name: uint24 type
references.description: Integer type that maps to ValueUnsigned32.

references.name: int24 type
references.description: Integer type that maps to ValueSigned32.

references.name: uint40 type
references.description: Integer type that maps to ValueUnsigned64.

references.name: int40 type
references.description: Integer type that maps to ValueSigned64.

references.name: uint48 type
references.description: Integer type that maps to ValueUnsigned64.

references.name: int48 type
references.description: Integer type that maps to ValueSigned64.

references.name: uint56 type
references.description: Integer type that maps to ValueUnsigned64.

references.name: int56 type
references.description: Integer type that maps to ValueSigned64.

references.name: Scenes Cluster
references.description: Provides scene storage and management capabilities, referenced by Scene Table Extensions in individual clusters.

references.name: Group Table
references.description: A table on the endpoint containing group identifiers, referenced by multiple commands for group verification. Likely managed by a Group Key Management Cluster (0x0004).

references.name: ExtensionFieldSetStruct
references.description: A structure definition for a set of attribute values for a cluster, used in scene definitions. Its structure definition is referenced but not provided in this section.

references.name: AttributeValuePairStruct
references.description: A structure containing an AttributeID and its Value, part of an ExtensionFieldSetStruct. Referenced in the AddScene command's effect on receipt.

references.name: On/Off Cluster
references.description: A Matter application cluster (ID 0x0006) that uses scene identifier 0 with group identifier 0 for a global scene, as mentioned in generic usage notes.

references.name: Interaction Model
references.description: The core Matter specification defining how commands are invoked and responses are generated (e.g., 'command was received as a unicast').
